data.shipland: Take control on ship to continue
data.scrap_descr: here and now, Sebastian, you have to make a choice, family or...
data.rockout: Warning! Local rock reserves are running low!
data.metalout: Warning! Local metal reserves are running low!
data.siliconout: Warning! Local silicon reserves are running low!
data.missionsdidntdone: first complete all missions on this planet
data.missionsdone: all missions completed, return to the ship
data.microscheme_descr: instantly activates upgrade selection when created
data.fruitdrone_descr: can carry 5 fruits or 1 two-handed cargo, flies to the ship and unloads when full
data.frdrav: you already have a delivery drone
data.generator_descr: required to power some devices, works at a short distance
data.vdetect_descr: marks infected creatures within a short distance, requires power
data.radar_descr: signals the nearest lost satellite, requires power
data.watercollector_descr: extracts fuel barrels from sources, can only be placed on a source, requires power
data.organiccollector_descr: extracts organic matter from vegetation into capsules, which decompose very quickly
data.organicav: you already have an organic collector
data.handscanner_descr: a small device that allows scanning flora and fauna, records fauna data in the bestiary
data.handscav: you already have a handheld scanner
data.lockpicker_descr: allows picking locks, breaks completely on a failed attempt
data.lockpickav: you already have an auto lockpick
data.turret_descr: a small helper turret, shoots at nearby creatures, requires power
data.barricade_descr: a wall made of crap and sticks, can withstand a few extra hits
data.hammer_descr: a small mallet that can awaken a dune worm, only works in dunes
data.barrel_descr: explodes when detonated, can cause a chain reaction, upgrades affect explosions
data.trap_descr: captures and locks a creature in a small box. Don't be a monster, they want to live too...
data.boombox_descr: plays various tunes and attracts the attention of local fauna
data.weedkiller_descr: a reactive mix of beer and rotten tomatoes! I wonder how it's made...
data.lightningrod_descr: a two-handed tool, sparks and attracts lightning during storms on forest planets
data.teleporter_descr: a portable two-handed teleporter, transports you to the next teleporter
data.cart_descr: allows storing and transporting two-handed items. Off to shopping we go!
data.arthive_descr: a small portable bee house, creates friendly bees ready to stand up for you, their honor and honey!
data.inhalant_descr: a canister of stimulating gas, increases running speed. However, it also has the opposite effect...
data.bonfire_descr: a very ordinary bonfire. And why do you need it if you have a spacesuit?
data.bestiaryscannew: creature entry added to the bestiary
data.ambushdetected: a wild fauna invasion is approaching!
data.deadnotif: press SPACE to go to the menu
data.expnotif: object explored. press SPACE to continue
data.winnotif: star system explored. press SPACE to end run
data.shipdidntbroken: you made a perfect landing without disturbing the local fauna!
data.shipbroken1: the ship is slightly damaged, requires 1 of scrap to repair
data.shipbroken2: the ship is damaged, requires 2 of scrap to repair
data.shipbroken3: the ship is much damaged, requires 3 of scrap to repair
data.shipbroken4: the ship is critically damaged, requires 5 of scrap to repair
data.didntplace0: this device can only be placed next to a generator!
data.didntplace1: the generator does not have enough energy to power this device!
data.didntplace2: this device can only be placed on the source!
data.hammerdune: hammer can only be used in the dune!
data.holde: hold E to board the ship
data.holdesc: hold ESC for 5 seconds to self kill and end the run
data.planetexplored1: this planet has already been explored
data.planetexplored2: this planet has already been explored
data.planetexplored3: this planet has already been explored
data.planetexplored4: this planet has already been explored
data.planetexplored6: I'm still not letting you in
data.planetexplored13: fine! go ahead, break it! we're millionaires - we'll buy a new one! >:(
data.moonexplored1: this moon has already been explored
data.moonexplored2: this moon has already been explored
data.moonexplored3: this moon has already been explored
data.moonexplored4: this moon has already been explored
data.moonexplored6: I'm still not letting you in
data.moonexplored13: fine! go ahead, break it! we're millionaires - we'll buy a new one! >:(
data.starlanding1: where do you think you're going?!
data.starlanding4: should we tell him you can't land on stars?
data.starlanding7: what exactly would you fly then?
data.starlanding11: you still hoping I'll let you through?
data.starlanding14: how many more times will you click this?
data.starlanding15: the button's already cracking because of you!
data.Pest1: Microscopic specimen the size of a large coin, primarily inhabiting weeds. Possesses high destructive capability.
data.Pest2: Coin-sized creature hiding in weed vegetation. Has disproportionately high destructive capacity.
data.Pest3: Tiny pest inhabiting weeds. Despite its size, capable of large-scale destruction.
data.Pest4: Small parasite infesting weed thickets. Compensates for microscopic dimensions with exceptional harmfulness.
data.Pest5: Nearly invisible organism living among weeds. Its destructive potential exceeds its size by orders of magnitude.
data.Pest6: Miniature pest camouflaged in weed grass. Possesses catastrophic destructive force relative to its size.
data.Pest7: Microscopic aggressor of weed plants. Causes disproportionately large damage compared to its body.
data.Pest8: Minuscule yet deadly pest inhabiting weeds. Its destructive abilities defy its modest dimensions.
data.Pest9: Weed parasite of coin diameter. Conceals colossal destructive potential within its microscopic body.
data.Pest10: Pest invisible to the naked eye in weed thickets. Its deceptive size hides immense destructive power.
data.Swarmer1: Miniature specimens (5-7 cm) exhibiting hypertrophied aggression immediately after hatching. Require no maturation period.
data.Swarmer2: Colonies demonstrate extreme aggressiveness when encountering any biologically foreign objects.
data.Swarmer3: Endoparasitic forms developing within the queen's body. Activated upon rupture of the chitinous cocoon capsule.
data.Swarmer4: Short-lived specimens (lifespan ~72 hours) with modified chelicerae for neurotoxin injection.
data.Swarmer5: Blind castoral mutants that navigate via chemoreception. Attack any organisms with differing pheromone profiles.
data.Swarmer6: Short-lived (up to 48 hours) specimens with chitinous spikes on limbs. Designed for high-density swarm attacks.
data.Swarmer7: Anaerobic variety capable of functioning in oxygen-deprived environments. Secrete corrosive substances when the exoskeleton is damaged.
data.Swarmer8: Swarm forms with second-order collective intelligence. Coordinate attacks via ultrasonic pulses.
data.Swarmer9: Biodegradable specimens. Upon death, their tissues release allelopathic substances that inhibit victim regeneration.
data.Swarmer10: Synchronized colonies functioning as a single organism. Each specimen acts as a biological 'tooth' within the collective system.
data.Friendly1: This species is characterized by high timidity, fleeing to cover at the slightest sign of danger.
data.Friendly2: With a timid disposition, representatives of this species strive to avoid any potential threats.
data.Friendly3: Extremely cautious creatures that prefer stealth and minimal interaction with their environment.
data.Friendly4: They exhibit innate wariness, hiding at any suspicious sound or movement.
data.Friendly5: They spend most of their lives in shadows, avoiding encounters with other organisms.
data.Friendly6: Their instincts are geared toward conflict avoidance, making them nearly invisible in their natural habitat.
data.Friendly7: They prefer solitude, rarely interacting even with members of their own species.
data.Friendly8: Possessing keen danger sense that allows them to hide from potential predators in advance.
data.Friendly9: They demonstrate an exceptionally reclusive lifestyle, relying on camouflage and quick reflexes.
data.Friendly10: Their survival mechanisms are based on avoiding confrontation, making them elusive in nature.
data.Neutral1: They show moderate caution but can fight back when necessary.
data.Neutral2: They prefer to avoid open conflicts but will defend their territory when threatened.
data.Neutral3: They balance between peaceful behavior and readiness for self-defense.
data.Neutral4: They don't show aggression without reason but can actively defend themselves.
data.Neutral5: They have a balanced temperament, avoiding conflicts unless necessary.
data.Neutral6: They live cautiously, assessing danger levels before deciding how to react.
data.Neutral7: Capable of adaptive behavior - retreating in some situations, showing aggression in others.
data.Neutral8: They remain peaceful in calm conditions but may show aggression when stressed.
data.Neutral9: Not prone to attack, but can show significant resistance when threatened.
data.Neutral10: Their behavior varies from neutral to defensive depending on circumstances.
data.Agressive1: They exhibit pronounced aggression toward potential opponents.
data.Agressive2: They show high predatory behavior, attacking without warning.
data.Agressive3: They have a warlike nature, constantly ready to attack.
data.Agressive4: They are active predators that view most creatures as potential prey.
data.Agressive5: Their natural aggression manifests in territorial behavior and frequent attacks on strangers.
data.Agressive6: They react sharply to boundary violations, immediately attacking intruders.
data.Agressive7: They can show aggression even without visible threat, attacking moving objects.
data.Agressive8: They live in constant competition, making them prone to attacks and territorial disputes.
data.Agressive9: They show extreme intolerance to outsiders, displaying immediate hostility.
data.Agressive10: Their dominant position in the ecosystem is maintained through aggressive and predatory lifestyle.
data.small1: They have miniature sizes comparable to small rodents.
data.small2: Their body length allows them to easily fit in a human palm.
data.small3: Small size makes them masters of camouflage in vegetation.
data.small4: Size helps them effectively evade predators and find hiding spots.
data.small5: So lightweight they exert minimal pressure on surfaces they move across.
data.small6: Their tiny bodies give them advantage when moving through narrow spaces.
data.small7: Miniature size makes them practically invisible to most predators.
data.small8: They prefer hidden lifestyle, using small size to escape threats.
data.small9: Small dimensions allow easy movement through complex terrain.
data.small10: Body length and lightness make them ideal inhabitants of dense vegetation.
data.medium1: Size comparable to medium-weight Earth dogs.
data.medium2: Dimensions provide balance between speed, strength and endurance.
data.medium3: Their bodies reach about one meter length, making them effective hunters.
data.medium4: Medium size lets them successfully compete with both larger and smaller species.
data.medium5: They have physique combining power and maneuverability.
data.medium6: They are versatile creatures adapted to wide range of conditions.
data.medium7: Body size allows effective use of both offensive and defensive strategies.
data.medium8: Large enough to dominate smaller species but inferior to bigger predators.
data.medium9: Physical characteristics enable successful hunting in challenging natural conditions.
data.medium10: Optimal size gives them advantages in speed and survivability.
data.large1: They have massive build and significant weight.
data.large2: Large size allows them to dominate most neighboring species.
data.large3: Their powerful bodies require extensive territory for foraging.
data.large4: Slow but possess impressive physical strength.
data.large5: Gigantic proportions make them nearly invulnerable to most predators.
data.large6: Their massive musculature allows overcoming significant obstacles.
data.large7: Adapted to living in conditions requiring high endurance.
data.large8: Huge size imposes limitations on movement speed.
data.large9: Massive limbs enable effective movement even across difficult terrain.
data.large10: They are among the largest creatures in their ecosystem.
data.clawed1: They primarily feed on flesh of other organisms, being active predators.
data.clawed2: They have specialized claws designed for grabbing and tearing prey.
data.clawed3: Powerful claws can pierce even hard protective coverings of victims.
data.clawed4: They use claws not only for hunting but also to demonstrate strength in territorial fights.
data.clawed5: They possess high claw grip strength, making them formidable predators.
data.clawed6: Claws play key role in their ecology, ensuring successful hunting.
data.clawed7: They use claws for defense against potential predators and competitors.
data.clawed8: Limb anatomy enables effective claw use in hunting and combat.
data.clawed9: They can deliver fast and lethal claw strikes.
data.clawed10: Natural armament makes them dangerous even to larger creatures.
data.clawless1: They feed exclusively on plants, showing no interest in predation.
data.clawless2: Lack specialized hunting limbs, being adapted for gathering fruits and herbs.
data.clawless3: Absence of claws is compensated by developed apparatus for chewing plant matter.
data.clawless4: Adapted for peaceful existence, obtaining food solely through grazing.
data.clawless5: Lack of claws makes them fully dependent on plant resource availability.
data.clawless6: Primarily herbivorous, consuming leaves, fruits and root vegetables.
data.clawless7: Their limbs are adapted for effective manipulation of plant food.
data.clawless8: Important ecosystem members acting as plant seed dispersers.
data.clawless9: Have specialized digestive tract adapted for fiber digestion.
data.clawless10: Specialize in high-fiber vegetation, avoiding competition with predators.
data.winged1: They possess flight capability thanks to developed wings.
data.winged2: Wings allow traveling significant distances searching for food and shelter.
data.winged3: Wing anatomy is adapted for maneuverable flight.
data.winged4: They fly using dynamic wing lift.
data.winged5: Wings give them strategic advantage over ground predators.
data.winged6: They travel between territories using air currents.
data.winged7: Can hover while observing potential prey.
data.winged8: Flight capability makes them less vulnerable to ground predators.
data.winged9: Developed wings enable long flights without significant energy expenditure.
data.winged10: Possess exceptional aerodynamics, making them among fastest creatures in their range.
data.wingless1: Completely lack flight capability and move exclusively on ground.
data.wingless2: Absence of wings is compensated by high running speed or developed camouflage.
data.wingless3: Adapted to terrestrial life with strong limbs for rapid movement.
data.wingless4: Movement occurs through jumps or swift running, reducing dependence on airspace.
data.wingless5: Their evolution led to complete wing loss, making them excellent ground predators or herbivores.
data.wingless6: Possess powerful limb musculature compensating for lack of flight.
data.wingless7: Evolutionarily adapted to surface life, occupying niches in various ecosystems.
data.wingless8: Use mobility and tactical maneuvering to avoid predators.
data.wingless9: Lack of wings makes them less weather-dependent compared to flying species.
data.wingless10: Have dense body structure excluding possibility of gliding or flight.
data.Plants0: The surface is predominantly covered with dense vegetation forming a continuous green cover.
data.Plants1: The landscape is characterized by dominant flora with rare patches of open ground.
data.Plants2: Vast territories are occupied by diverse forms of plant life creating a dense biomass.
data.Plants3: Nearly all visible surfaces are covered with plants of various species and sizes.
data.Plants4: Plant communities form a complex multi-layered structure covering most of the territory.
data.Plants5: Green cover extends to the horizon, only occasionally interrupted by small clearings.
data.Plants6: Flora demonstrates extensive overgrowth, leaving almost no space for other landscape forms.
data.Plants7: Vegetation formations constitute the basis of the local biome, shaping the region's distinctive appearance.
data.Plants8: Biological mass is represented mainly by photosynthetic organisms of various types.
data.Plants9: The ecosystem is distinguished by a predominance of autotrophic organisms creating continuous plant coverage.
data.Rocks0: The landscape consists primarily of exposed rock formations with rich mineral deposits and almost complete absence of vegetation.
data.Rocks1: The surface represents an accumulation of mineral formations of various compositions with extremely scarce flora.
data.Rocks2: Rock masses rich in ore deposits dominate the area where signs of plant life are minimal.
data.Rocks3: The territory demonstrates geological diversity of rocks with high concentrations of valuable minerals but is practically lifeless biologically.
data.Rocks4: The landscape is formed by chaotic accumulations of rocks with industrially significant inclusions where plants are almost never found.
data.Rocks5: Mineral-rich terrain with extensive bedrock outcrops characterized by extremely poor vegetation.
data.Rocks6: The surface abounds with geological formations containing high concentrations of minerals while remaining nearly devoid of flora.
data.Rocks7: Rocky plains and stone formations rich in resource deposits form a vegetation-free biotope.
data.Rocks8: Dominance of lithospheric structures with industrially valuable rocks coexists with nearly complete absence of phytomass.
data.Rocks9: The landscape represents a mineralogical complex with developed ore base where biological life is limited to isolated plants.
data.Dune0: Monotonous sand arrays stretch uniformly to the horizon.
data.Dune1: The surface is completely hidden under a powerful sandy mantle leaving no patches of open ground.
data.Dune2: Deep sand deposits form an absolutely flat, uninterrupted relief.
data.Dune3: The territory represents a continuous sand field without visible boundaries or transitions.
data.Dune4: Multimeter sand strata completely determine the character of the terrain.
data.Dune5: Ubiquitous distribution of loose sand masses creates an effect of absolute geological homogeneity.
data.Dune6: An exceptionally thick sand cover masks all features of the underlying surface.
data.Dune7: The landscape demonstrates absolute dominance of aeolian sand deposits.
data.Dune8: A continuous sand layer devoid of any solid inclusions forms a featureless relief.
data.Dune9: Powerful sand beds uninterrupted by other geological formations define the region's appearance.
data.Crystal0: Extensive crystalline formations demonstrate properties of a unified neural network responding to external influences coherently.
data.Crystal1: A complex system of interconnected crystals shows signs of collective intelligence functioning as a biological computer.
data.Crystal2: Living mineral structures form an analogue of a distributed nervous system on a planetary scale.
data.Crystal3: The crystalline surface behaves as a single organism demonstrating complex reactions to stimuli.
data.Crystal4: Self-replicating crystals form analogues of neural circuits processing information through geometric patterns.
data.Crystal5: Electrically conductive crystal arrays transmit impulses mimicking synaptic connections in a giant biomineral system.
data.Crystal6: Dynamic crystal lattices function as a quantum processor where each element is part of a thinking structure.
data.Crystal7: Fractal crystalline formations demonstrate memristor properties in a planetary neuromorphic system.
data.Crystal8: Light-conducting mineral networks transmit information at speeds comparable to nerve impulses in complex organisms.
data.Crystal9: Collective behavior of crystal clusters reproduces principles of neuroglia functioning at landscape scales.
data.Ice0: Boundless icy expanses strewn with massive fossil ice blocks are occasionally interrupted by islands of hardy vegetation.
data.Ice1: The frozen landscape presents a chaotic accumulation of ice formations with rare inclusions of cryophytic flora.
data.Ice2: Glacial structures dominate the area where only isolated patches show traces of plant life adapted to extreme conditions.
data.Ice3: Giant ice masses form a harsh terrain where few plants exist in a state of permanent suspended animation.
data.Ice4: The cryolithozone with numerous ice inclusions supports only minimal biological activity of specialized plant forms.
data.Ice5: Permafrost adorned with sculpted ice formations permits existence of only the hardiest vegetation species.
data.Ice6: An icy landscape consisting of intricate crystalline structures is occasionally interrupted by islands of inhibited plant life.
data.Ice7: A glacial landscape with chaotic distribution of ice blocks contains microzones with cryophilic plant communities.
data.Ice8: Perennial ice formations determine the territory's appearance where only specialized plants can survive in narrow ecological niches.
data.Ice9: A frozen desert dominated by ice structures sustains existence of only minimal plant biomass in cryopreservation state.
data.Hive0: The surface is covered with giant wax honeycombs, with chitinous stems and transparent wings replacing plants.
data.Hive1: Mountain ranges are replaced by hive megastructures, riddled with tunnels and resonating with low humming.
data.Hive2: The landscape consists of hardened honey and wax formations resembling stone boulders.
data.Hive3: Chitinous bushes with vibrating wings mimic vegetation, generating weak sound waves.
data.Hive4: The surface is a continuous array of honeycomb cells with mobile winged appendages swaying above them.
data.Hive5: Stone boulders are actually large solid hives whose cavities are filled with thick pheromone mist.
data.Hive6: Photosynthesis here is performed by chitinous towers with pulsating membranes that transform light into biomass.
data.Hive7: The surface is a mixture of crystallized nectar and living chitinous structures.
data.Hive8: The planet's entire geometry follows a hexagonal grid, like a giant natural matrix.
data.Hive9: The atmosphere is filled with pheromones and the synchronized hum of countless wings.
data.Meat0: The surface is a unified biological mass of tightly interwoven muscle fibers, with bone-like structures growing between them.
data.Meat1: Giant pulsating mounds form the terrain, their membranous structure resembling giant follicles.
data.Meat2: Bony formations grow in ordered clusters, serving to stabilize the living surface.
data.Meat3: The planet exhibits properties of a unified muscular system - wave-like contractions periodically ripple across the surface.
data.Meat4: Local vegetation analogs consist of mineralized protein structures forming complex branching patterns.
data.Meat5: The terrain is shaped by cyclical swelling and contraction of giant dermal formations.
data.Meat6: The biological landscape is maintained by constant metabolism - the surface secretes organic substrate and absorbs nutrients.
data.Meat7: Bony 'trees' are actually protrusions of the planet's internal skeleton piercing through the muscle layer.
data.Meat8: All geological processes are replaced by biological ones - instead of tectonic shifts, rhythmic contractions of muscle strata are observed.
data.Meat9: The surface reacts to external stimuli with local spasms, demonstrating properties of a unified organism.
data.Mushroom0: The entire surface is a continuous mycelial array, dotted with fruiting bodies of giant mushrooms.
data.Mushroom1: The planet is covered in an endless mycelium, strewn with huge fungal fruiting bodies.
data.Mushroom2: The surface is entirely covered with mycelium, upon which gigantic mushrooms grow.
data.Mushroom3: Giant mushrooms grow throughout the territory, forming a single mycelial cover.
data.Mushroom4: A powerful mycelial layer, studded with fruiting bodies of enormous mushrooms, forms the planet's surface.
data.Mushroom5: A continuous carpet of mycelium, covered with giant mushrooms, stretches to the horizon.
data.Mushroom6: A mycelial network with numerous fruiting bodies of huge mushrooms dominates everywhere.
data.Mushroom7: The planetary surface is a giant mycelium with massive growth of large mushrooms.
data.Mushroom8: Vast expanses are occupied by mycelium, where huge mushrooms are commonly found.
data.Mushroom9: A unified mycelial array, covered with fruiting bodies of giant mushrooms, shapes the planet's landscape.
data.moonsize0_0: Miniature satellite whose extremely small dimensions prevent spherical shape formation, resembling rather an irregular cosmic pebble.
data.moonsize0_1: Tiny celestial body with gravity too weak to achieve rounded contours maintains an amorphous potato-like form.
data.moonsize0_2: Dwarf satellite with characteristic deformed geometry typical for subcritical mass objects.
data.moonsize0_3: Small astronomical object whose irregular shape reveals insufficient gravitational influence for spherification.
data.moonsize0_4: Ultraminiature cosmic body whose morphology resembles rather a shapeless asteroid than a classic satellite.
data.moonsize0_5: Gravitationally unstable mini-body retaining irregular outlines due to insufficient mass for self-correction of form.
data.moonsize0_6: Subplanetary object with pronounced structural asymmetry characteristic for bodies below hydrostatic equilibrium limit.
data.moonsize0_7: Dwarf satellite whose improper configuration indicates inability to overcome gravitational rounding threshold.
data.moonsize0_8: Ultralow-mass astronomical formation demonstrating typical geological amorphousness for such cases.
data.moonsize0_9: Celestial body in transitional stage between asteroid and satellite retaining characteristic shape irregularity due to insufficient gravity.
data.moonsize1_0: Large celestial body of spherical shape possessing significant mass and own gravity.
data.moonsize1_1: Massive cosmic object whose dimensions allow maintaining geological activity.
data.moonsize1_2: Giant satellite with clearly expressed layered structure and complex surface relief.
data.moonsize1_3: Object of substantial size demonstrating signs of interior differentiation and atmospheric phenomena.
data.moonsize1_4: Large-scale body possessing sufficient gravity for stable spherical form formation.
data.moonsize1_5: Cosmic formation of considerable size with own tectonic and possibly atmospheric dynamics.
data.moonsize1_6: Massive astronomical object showing features of complex internal structure and active surface.
data.moonsize1_7: Gravitationally significant body whose physical parameters exceed typical for most satellites.
data.moonsize1_8: Extensive celestial body with pronounced geological history and diverse surface topography.
data.moonsize1_9: Cosmic formation of exceptional size demonstrating characteristics usually inherent to more massive objects.
data.planetsize0_0: Tiny planetary body with minimal dimensions and barely perceptible gravitational field.

data.planetsize0_3: Planetoidal object of ultraminiature scale possessing minuscule gravitational force.
data.planetsize0_4: Subplanetary body whose parameters are borderline between asteroid and full-fledged planet.
data.planetsize0_5: Gravitationally insignificant planetary-type formation with record-small dimensions.
data.planetsize0_6: Astronomical body of extremely small caliber barely meeting planetary criteria.
data.planetsize0_7: Planetary midget whose physical characteristics approach the lower possible limit.
data.planetsize0_8: Ultracompact celestial body demonstrating only trace signs of gravitational influence.
data.planetsize0_9: Microscopic by planetary standards object with correspondingly weak gravity.
data.planetsize1_0: Small planet whose size is at the lower boundary of planetary class.

data.planetsize1_2: Planet of modest dimensions where gravity only slightly exceeds lunar.
data.planetsize1_3: Small planetary object with diameter slightly smaller than Mars.
data.planetsize1_4: Small spherical body with gravity sufficient for orbit clearing but insufficient for dense atmosphere.
data.planetsize1_5: Planet occupying intermediate position between dwarf planets and full worlds.
data.planetsize1_6: Miniature but full-fledged planetary object with gravity sufficient for geological activity.
data.planetsize1_7: Compact spherical world whose dimensions allow classification as minor planet.
data.planetsize1_8: Planet with reduced mass where mountain systems may reach anomalously high elevations relative to diameter.
data.planetsize1_9: Small but geologically complex planetary body with clearly expressed interior differentiation.

data.faunasize0_0: Fauna is represented by extremely limited species set with most ecological niches empty.

data.faunasize0_2: An almost monocultural ecosystem where only few adaptable organisms survived.
data.faunasize0_3: Fauna experienced mass extinction leaving only pitiful remnants of former diversity.

data.faunasize0_5: An ecosystem in decline - most specialized species disappeared leaving only most undemanding.
data.faunasize0_6: Biological uniformity where even traces of former species richness are hard to find.
data.faunasize0_7: Faunistic minimum - surviving species barely maintain fragile ecological balance.
data.faunasize0_8: Practically sterile habitat with isolated examples of animal life.
data.faunasize0_9: A crippled ecosystem that lost majority of its biological components.

data.faunasize1_3: Unrich fauna where ecological niches are distributed between few dominant organisms.
data.faunasize1_4: A food chain of medium complexity with pronounced leading species.
data.faunasize1_5: Moderately poor fauna with noticeable domination of most adapted species.
data.faunasize1_6: An ecosystem with basic set of animals where some groups gained clear advantage.
data.faunasize1_7: Limited biodiversity with couple of clearly numerically dominant species.
data.faunasize1_8: Uncomplicated faunistic structure with pronounced dominants and few specialists.
data.faunasize1_9: Sufficiently uniform animal environment with several clearly predominant taxa.

data.duneworm_descr: Giant dune worms belong to the annelid class and play a critical role in the ecosystem. Through cyclical vertical migration, they consume local fauna. In turn, their secreted compounds form stabilizing structures with hardness comparable to sedimentary rock, preventing sand collapses. A unique feature of their reproductive process is budding, where each parent produces a single successor (rarely two) before instantly perishing.
data.cellarworm_descr: The origin of culvert worms remains a mystery. The prevailing theory suggests they evolved from dune worms via xenogenic adaptation during a failed terraforming experiment in the past. Some researchers believe culvert worms possess biological teleportation mechanisms, explaining their presence in abandoned drainage systems across disparate planets.
data.manorghost_descr: Scientists report recurring encounters between researchers and spectral entities within anomalous wooden structures scattered across worlds. These architectural artifacts—crafted from identical xenowood of unknown origin—are presumed relics of a vanished advanced civilization. One hypothesis posits that compounds emitted by the material induce collective visual hallucinations, casting doubt on the ghosts' physical reality.
data.watcher_descr: Debates persist over whether the Watcher qualifies as a traditional organism. The crystalline planets and moons they inhabit function as vast neural networks, suggesting the Watcher may be an extension of the landscape itself rather than a discrete entity. Morphologically resembling a human eye, Watchers manifest randomly to surveil their surroundings. Any lifeform caught in their gaze is ruthlessly eliminated.
data.giantbee_descr: Unlike other types of bees, hermits do not live in swarms, but alone. They are quite aggressive by nature - any unwanted movement can attract their attention and start an attack. There is an assumption that hermit bees are not a different species at all, but a special stage of development of ordinary bees, which is possible only on honey planets under certain conditions.
data.devourer_descr: The meat planets themselves and their origin are a real mystery for science, as are the eaters that live on them. Outwardly, they look like a mouth with sharp teeth, consisting of a material that is very similar to ordinary iron, and can be of different sizes. To date, there is no scientific explanation for the nature of the eaters, but they disappear and appear quite quickly. This may indicate that the eaters are just a reaction of meat planets to external irritants, like ordinary human scabies.
data.rockfall_descr: Massive collapses of boulders are extremely common on rocky planets. Erupting volcanoes eject enormous amounts of giant stones into the sky. Upon landing, they obliterate all life and occasionally bring valuable minerals from the depths.
data.storm_descr: Storms periodically engulf forest planets, and at first glance may resemble typical Earth thunderstorms. However, unknown atmospheric anomalies can alter the chemical composition of precipitation. Accompanied by lightning, these rains create pools of random aggressive compounds on the surface that can be deadly. Despite their short duration, such storms cause significant harm to local wildlife and drastically reduce fauna populations.
data.blizzard_descr: Short-lived storms that abruptly alter environmental conditions upon appearance. Visibility drops to near zero. Tiny ice crystals form in the air, creating an instant temperature drop to anomalously low values and restricting mobility. When thermal balance is restored, the blizzard ceases immediately.
data.balllightning_descr: A rare phenomenon that can appear in the place where ordinary lightning strikes. Ball lightning is very dangerous and unpredictable. Moving extremely chaotically, it is capable of destroying everything that gets in its way. It is noteworthy that the composition of ball lightning is completely identical to the composition of precipitation, which can give it even greater destructive power. Ball lightning usually disappears over time, but the duration of its existence can vary greatly.
data.meteorshower_descr: A rare and very dangerous astronomical phenomenon in which dozens of small space bodies extend through the atmosphere and crash into the surface. This occurs when the planet passes through a dense stream of asteroids, whose fragments do not have time to completely burn up during the fall. Scientists believe that meteor showers are the result of ancient cosmic catastrophes, when large space bodies, as a result of destruction, leave behind a trail of debris in orbit.
data.sporozoan_descr: A unique organism that is a symbiosis of a large spotted fungus and a photosynthetic vine. Its elastic, rubber-like thallus is capable of instant and strong stretching, thanks to unique protein fibrils. After hunting, the sporozoan returns to the mycelial network, leaving behind a small number of fungal spores.
data.plasmodium_descr: A shapeless fleshy body, devoid of signs of centralized nervous activity. It moves chaotically with the help of spontaneous muscle contractions. A characteristic property of plasmodium is their ability to detonate when capturing low-frequency vibrations. Many researchers, Those who have seen this creature believe that it is a biological weapon, but there is no scientific evidence to support this.
data.explorerghost_descr: I came back... empty-handed and loaded with... bad luck. They don't answer, do they? Base... Earth... No one answers. I'm trying to tell you this... not to hurt you, but to tell you. Look at me. I am your future. The one who brought the end of the world in his ship. Not poison... not a weapon... a quiet, small passenger. Invisible. And when you return... you will be me.
data.battlerover_descr: A small combat robot of unknown origin. Armed with a double-barreled laser weapon, the outer appearance of which resembles binoculars, which likely also serve as some kind of eye. Its operating system is easily reconfigured, allowing it to be used as a temporary ally. However, before confirmation, it must be neutralized.
